txt: *Before* recorded history, men and women were able to do simple, very primitive things together sober. Acknowledge the situation -- and keep it basic.
*Feelings* will guide the way.
*Try The Expression*? C'mon, why are you still doing this?
... use it to take a dig at his friend. Flood gates open.
...after all you've done -- is what her tone implies.
...open fire -- we can't have that. Interfere, now!
...or can you give him a moment? Somehow you realize this is not going to net you any *professional discounts*. Already he's reverting back to defensive.
A counterargument, but still -- your historic mass-murderer fantasies have only endeared you to this person.
A form of communication where words have failed?
A little too far there -- but you're definitely right.
A man this *chill* is at least a triple-digit billionaire.
A tool for manipulation. Give the lowest amount and people will be ingratiated to you.
A tough nut to crack, for sure. But the strongest containers often have the softest hearts.
A vanity he wouldn't mind.
A whore, huh? A lot of that going around lately. From the mouths of *distressed* men. Whatever the case, you've hit a nerve. He's angry -- no, more than that -- he feels *betrayed*.
Abigail is his wife or girlfriend. Chances are, she's gone. Calling her wouldn't make it any better or worse.
Alcohol-man? That's *exactly* what she needs! Now make her see that.
All qualities these two seem to share in abundance.
All right. You're in. Begin with the first one -- you'll appear *worldly*.
Almost! He almost burst out there. Keep piling arguments, anything...
Almost. But you need *one* more. Just one. Or maybe two...
An acorn is not the same as the tree. That requires time, diligence, and care...
And *why* did you think that was a good idea? Don't listen to me -- I'm wrong *all the time*.
And now he longs to see her covered in blood.
As if you've committed some faux pas.
As soon as this conversation's done, *you* put on the Fuck-the-World jacket. We'll take it from there...
Ask her about lodgings...
Ask her about the cool sword -- helps to break the ice.
At this point, plain badgering may be your best bet.
Be careful. Ask something else first. When you go *there*, use words like 'I hear you have been through something difficult.'
Be friendly.
Be honest. He's not grilling you, he just wants to know. Ask if he's ever wanted to take it too.
Because you know each other. She's been talking to you so openly because you've talked before.
Better keep the lieutenant in the dark about the whole 'Icebreaker' thing. He's not gonna get it.
Big talk, but that leaf compactor won't leave his mind any time soon. It's a small thing -- but it got him off centre...
Big talk, but you got him to admit he's a bad leader. It's a small thing -- but it got him off centre...
Bingo! This specimen has surrounded himself not only with one or two, but *three* stellar babes.
Brilliant negotiating there, detective -- as always!
Brother, you should put me in front of a firing squad. I have no words for how I failed you.
But he does *not* approve.
But it *has*, hasn't it? A seed can only bear what's inside it. The seed of love is black and oily.
But... she wants to tell you.
C'mon. Just a little. You're buddies.
Can you eat honour? Are you an *honour-vore?* Give me a break...
Coax it out of him... he's still warm. They were fucking...
Conversation might bring a discount, no?
Could be, but even more importantly -- women. This specimen has surrounded himself not only with one or two, but *three* stellar babes.
Could it be that he just doesn't want to discuss a police raid with another police officer?
Could that have been part of the reason they hanged him?
Cuno appreciates you recognizing him as the sovereign of Jamrock, though... North Jamrock, that is.
Cuno? Cunoesse? Two of a kind.
Curiosity for the Union burns brighter in her than curiosity for the case...
Damn, I thought the size thing would work...
Damn, you almost had it. What was this -- a pang of conscience?
Damn. It didn't work. You should have signalled you're a nationalist before.
Dangerous! Ask about him first. You don't want personal facts about his dead friend coming out of your mouth -- *he* has to start it.
Definitely no small talk, this isn't the time or place.
Did that just cheer him up a bit? Could be... It feels like the lieutenant cracked a little smile there.
Does she also *party* with Ruby, however?
Don't be *paranoid*, man. It's unbecoming of a police officer -- and it makes you sweat.
Don't be tactful. She doesn't care.
Don't even try to win him over. You won't.
Don't go all *bad cop* on this simple friendly fellow.
Don't go straight for the kill, exhaust everything else first, soften him up...
Don't mess this up, remember -- he *wants* to tell you. Get personal.
Don't say *funny* things. He looks calm, but these are serious accusations against -- against one of their own.
Don't say anything about the glasses. He's very sensitive about them.
Don't worry. We will protect you from her beauty. We will *consult* you through the reefs and sounds of her persona.
Doubtful. Women however... He's surrounded himself not only with one or two, but *three* stellar babes.
Easy -- when her research is done, she can move out.
Even after all that hell he's still bitter about her?
Even if it comes to a fight it's *always* a good idea to drag it out first. Get under his skin.
Even if it comes to a fight it's *always* a good idea to drag it out. Get under his skin.
Even if it comes to a fight it's a good idea to get under his skin first.
Ever better! It will further destabilize him -- costing time and maybe even lives!
Everyone wants to talk about themselves. She will, in time.
Finally, you got something out of him. This could prove useful in the future.
Flattery is the way.
For a moment, his antipathy is overcome by compassion for the sheer smallness of the money.
Forgive her.
Get the drink ON. It would be unimaginable for a human female to have sex with a man without Al-Gul as the master of ceremonies to guide them.
Good choice. A newer photo would look... different.
Good one, Cuno. It doesn't have to hold in court. Just establish a line of sight and he'll do the rest himself.
Good. I like this. This guy is learning.
Good. Step one complete. Now, let the lieutenant feast his eyes on the fruit of the tree of cool.
Guess.
Hang on, she's a professional *negotiator*. She should be open to some sort of mutually beneficial arrangement.
Have you tried... goading him?
Having the lieutenant along is a fail-safe measure against any possible funny-business.
He also really doesn't want to talk about it.
He did it! He said *Wompty-Dompty-Dom Centre* like it's the most natural thing in the world.
He didn't like him -- that only makes it worse.
He didn't think you were about to bust a case.
He does not seem to be overly thankful of your kindness as he hangs up.
He does not sound very convinced anymore.
He finds your lack of historic knowledge troubling. A sign of mental deterioration in the proceeding generations.
He gets it, you lost it. Just don't dig the hole any deeper.
He gets it. You lost. Now walk away with what remains of your dignity.
He has an idea who *she* is. Many cops have a *she* or *he* who left. But he just doesn't know what to say.
He is trying to justify it to himself.
He just needs some convincing before you can talk to him mano a mano.
He means it. The moment your jacket comes off, so does his. That means you're probably going to have to keep fucking the world till the end of days.
He probably just doesn't like her.
He really doesn't like it being Klaasje.
He really wants you to realize that he was also on the balcony looking by. In the *danger* zone, so to say.
He really went out of his way to seem comfortable with this topic. That's all you're gonna get for now.
He reminds him of himself. The same hatred. The same... you try to think of another thing -- but no, it's just the hatred.
He repeats the name with care. As if it were at risk of breaking.
He seems down on himself. Offer a bit of positivity and you might put him in a more benevolent mood.
He seems genuinely surprised at your response. In a good way.
He seems reluctant to talk about himself, but he'll open up -- if you prod a little.
He still refuses to believe you subscribe to Mazovian socio-economics, but entertaining the thought has given him some measure of solace.
He wanted her to see the man for what he was. Now that you know, you might wanna lay off this topic -- or else you might antagonize him.
He wants to see her covered in blood.
He winks at you, trying to relay some hidden message. Inviting you to mispronounce it too perhaps? It's odd.
He would be appreciative if you did not further chase this line of inquire in front of the women.
He would really, *really* get riled up if you said:
He's *aching* to get back to his usual shtick. All this whispering is bad for business.
He's a chatty guy. *Wants* to talk about the strike. Return once you've met the Union boss, and are on a better footing with the organization.
He's actually glad it's addressed now.
He's anything but a loser. Although he would like to be seen as one. It's cooler that way.
He's been sweet-talked by this Edgar. They must've met in person for such animosity to have developed.
He's exhausted from years of dealing with the *assholes*. That's why he's trying so hard not to be one too...
He's genuinely impressed, but that's mostly to do with how scientific that percentage sounded.
He's not sorry about his behaviour for your sake. Now that you have money, he really wants to make sure you're not angry with him.
He's right. Don't start guessing. Now's not a good time.
He's thinking of a way to gain some advantage from his embarrassing situation.
He's trying to act dismissive, but at this particular moment, he's more open than usual to the idea of paranatural phenomena.
He's trying to ingratiate himself with you by anticipating your questions. He knows he shouldn't have sold the gun.
Hear that, hero? Feels good, doesn't it? You should relay confidential information more often.
Hear that? You did it the honest way. You got the *real* signatures and now he's happy. Well done.
Hearing it may destabilize him -- you sure you've gotten everything from him?
Her voice is absolutely sizzling with excitement.
Her voice is surprisingly calm.
Here's a man who knows how to play the media -- by starting with the right question.
Hey, hey, what are you doing -- laying everything out like that?!
Hey, maybe if you were to find out more about her previous company, Fortress Accident, you'd have something new to say...
Hey, so we've been monitoring you internally, and now we know your *copotype*.
Hey, what makes the world go round?
His boyfriend is on his way home. He brings tins of meat and vegetables with him. Their pockets are heavier with money; but only slightly.
Hmm, it's a decent start, but you might need to change strategies down the line...
Hmmm... This one does not *like* Ruby. May prove useful.
Humiliation wasn't enough for her? How about you offer her something -- like a job?
I *told* you not to say that. This is not a joking matter for him.
I mean favours for favours. A *dirty* alliance of some sort.
If you can't even wear it outside for everyone to see then yes, time to abandon rock and roll.
If you want to know more about Ruby, this is a dead end. She's only heard the word 'lady' and latched onto it. You can push on if you're interested in *that*, though.
In another time and place, she might have flirted back. But that ship sailed long ago.
In shape, out of shape. You've always preferred to rely on your wits.
Is it possible to *hear* someone blush? Because you're sure that's what she's doing...
It could have been a *desire* murder. Maybe an act of jealousy?
It doesn't have to hold in court. Just establish a line of sight and he'll do the rest himself.
It feels good, it feels right... but what is this? What is this *thing* that Andre is doing with his limbs?!
It has a taste *you're* quite familiar with.
It only lasts for the briefest instance but you catch a look of disappointment in her eye. Anger even.
It really took courage. Don't pick at him.
It sounds like she tried looking into it herself, though she's clearly not the type your typical lorryman would confide in.
It sounds like she tried looking into it herself. But she's clearly not the type your typical lorryman would confide in.

It was a good idea. But she felt obliged by the Headless FALN Rider -- to give you things in return. Things she no longer wants to give you. So she refused. That's how it goes -- your figurine rider idea was *naive*.
It will only last a day or two, a week tops. Then her mood will sour and she will feel *the curse* again.
It's a *miracle* no one got shot this time. Stop attempting to use your *head*. It won't work!
It's a secret handshake of the ultra rich, distributed though private-access yacht clubs and interisolary balls for the heirs and heiresses of the old money. Where you picked it up is a mystery.
It's a sign!
It's almost like he's worried for you.
It's clear she *liked* it.
It's downright *incriminating*. He has little choice but to give you one, although he really does not want to.
It's hard to get a read on her precise disposition, but she *appears* helpful.
It's hard to get a read on the lieutenant. What would he do if you stole them? Would he report you, or not? Surprisingly, you feel like he probably wouldn't.
It's not *that* okay. Get off this subject. Now.
It's not hard to see at all. You hand out drugs to kids -- the lieutenant's faith in your judgement will diminish significantly.
It's obvious -- you need to steer the conversation to the topic that interests him *the most*. Something that he'd like to discuss with another man, in private...
It's okay, she just hasn't figured out how these things work between the sexes. Keep at it. She'll come around.
It's okay. She's emotional right now. Keep at it, wear her out.
It's quite imaginable. You start handing drugs out to kids -- the lieutenant's faith in your judgement will diminish significantly.
It's really in her best interest to listen to you. You *know* how the world works.
It's tough to tell, but he actually does... a little. He tilts his head to the right, reassessing you in the light of your bad ass learning disability.
It's true. She was the only thing holding him back.
It's true. You have a problem.
Judging by his attitude -- probably the second one.
Judging from how happy he is, it looks like you did it. He doesn't *appear* to suspect trickery.
Just be straight with him.
Just dodge the first shot -- and the second will be easier. Drunks are quick to anger -- and make mistakes.
Just don't antagonize him, you have this already.
Just don't ask him for the smallest amount. You'll insult him. The others will work.
Just don't say you don't have *any* answer yet. The uncertainty is killing her.
Just explain why you need this so much. He's bound to understand.
Just keep her talking and you'll get through this.
Just one more, it can't be more than one now...
Just talk. It'll smooth things over. Old people like attention.
Just tell her about Egg Head's defence speech.
Keep talking about the head-measuring man. Politics yields information from this man, gets him spilling facts.
Layman? You always have to prove yourself with this man. Which means -- *you* have to provide the cryptids. *Maybe* he'll answer.
Like ocean waves shaping the coastline, you will wear her down. Pebble by pebble. Just be persistent.
Listen, despite his hostile exterior, this one's got a soft underbelly. He's desperate for affirmation...
Logic and reason won't work on this old bat. Better go for shameless emotional manipulation. What's a grandmother's deepest vulnerability?
Manipulating his patriotic impulses like that? René is gonna remember that.
Massage his ego, feed him a few grapes, make him *like* you.
Maybe don't tell people the people they don't like are actually right...
Maybe it's best to tell her the truth after all. Here we go...
Maybe just look at her with big pleading eyes?
Maybe some general remarks before you say something big? Work your way up to the *cool*.
Maybe the carrier wanted to impress his peers... demonstrate his physical might?
Maybe the speedfreaks can help you with this? Go talk with Andre.
Maybe there was a physically impressive strongman in their midst. Someone who wanted to impress their peers.
Maybe you should just stand there, watch him rattle his toolbox. Get to know *his* 'sine.'
More than some. He admires this about her.
More. He's gonna crack, but you need a little more.
More. More questions before doing anything.
Move on. Don't gloat.
Nicking an idea that wasn't worth anything in the first place and trying to pass it off as your own? How very *entrepreneurial*. I love it.
No need to act out of line here. It may even be wise to bow your head out of respect.
No need to overdo it. He won't mind sharing it with you.
No one likes to talk about their dirty laundry and their empty bottles. Maybe if you phrase it emotionally...
No! Don't say it. Don't beg. It will only make her *go*!
No, first ask an innocuous personal question to get the interview off on the right foot.
No, just say you're honest with him, don't *challenge* him.
No, no, don't listen to *the stiff*. Be cool.
No, no, no! You've sensitivity tested this!
No, the thunder is his. Leave it to the lieutenant.
No, you don't. C'mon. You'll be back to saying sorry in two minutes. Stop wasting time and begin The Repentance.
No, you're the *Sorry Cop*. The cop who's sorriest. Let's make it official, then, shall we?
No. We can do this. Drag it out a bit. Get under his skin.
No. We can do this. Drag it out a little. Get under his skin.
No... don't 'rip into' anyone. You're sensitive, remember? Communicate.
None that you can see.
Nonsense, no one 'repels wealth', the businessmen just don't know how to entice them.
Nope, you don't have any new winning tactics to get the woman to spill the beans about cryptids.
Not a big fan of scepticism, this one.
Not bad.
Not terribly impressed, not surprised either.
Now is not a good time to ask him about the corpse-fridge.
Now is the time to be humble. Say the *sir* thing.
Now the man feels embarrassed. Time to move on perhaps?
Now watch the other guys fuck it up by falling out of line.
Now! You got them both off-piste! He's foaming mad at her...
Of course you are. It's okay. See if you can get something out of this. Like... info, or... maybe every time you say you're sorry you get a million bucks?
Of course! Nothing bad will happen. Talk about his dead friend! He seems stable enough to take it.
Of course, if being outside, looking like a rock-lion is making you 'feel silly', then just drop the whole thing.
Of course. This case takes precedence.
Oh no, she's making excuses. You're not interesting enough to talk cryptids with.
Oh, the need *will* arise. Here's how we'll do it:
Oh, yes she does -- you need to get your drink on. There is no other way for human beings to procreate. Not after six thousand years of yeast cultivation-based mating rituals.
Oh, you know. Apocalypse, Superstellar, The Advanced Interesting Cop. Liquid Shadow Cop. But you're too sorry to say those things, so -- here we go?
Oil for the wheels. Much needed in stressful times like these.
Okay, don't say you need to 'talk' right away. Melt the ice first -- this way you're *already* talking.
Okay, just for the length of this short demonstration, please say something that isn't *I'm sorry*. Guess. What is it?
Okay, she's actually *more* agitated now. My bad.
Okay, this turned out better than expected.
Okay, yes. Immediate failure. Things are worse now. He's observing your every motion carefully...
Okay. Good.
Okay. Well. Maybe I was wrong. We've all learned something today.
On the surface he's dismissive, but there's a conspiratorial glint in his black eyes. If only you knew what *he* would say...
Oops. Well, no one said it would work. Back to detecting now.
Or an admirer?
Othen than race science, what else do you think he's into?
Perfect, all you have to do is *pretend* to have taste. This will be your weapon when swindling artists for their overpriced 'work'.
Plaisance is not going to like what you have to tell her.
Probably not. Women however... He's surrounded himself not with one or two, but *three* stellar babes.
Really? None of this looks like it's going to do anything but piss him off.
Remember -- the island isn't going anywhere. It's not like it's going to *sail away* or *disappear*. Take it easy!
Rent-a-cop? So that's what this is about. He doesn't see you as his equal.
Rent-a-cop? So that's what this is about. He doesn't see you as his equals.
René must have been quite a loss for him. Maybe you could still get him to talk about things that don't require as much rage... because it's not there anymore.
SAY MY NAME BITCH! What are you doing, you sound way too aggressive.
SUGGESTION
Say something. Get off this trail. Thank god it's only *ineffectual* thus far.
See, even he acknowledges that it's a good reason!
See? He doesn't like the *vibration* from the red chair either.
See? He's doing the hard work himself. All he needed was a little nudge...
She *transforms* when speaking about these strange animals -- into a confident woman.
She actually is impressed, this is more like a defensive reflex.
She appears aloof, but that scar part... the *scary* is stressed and drawn out. What's that about? Apprehension?
She clearly doesn't think she's a 'lady'. Don't repeat it.
She clearly enjoyed saying that to you.
She clearly enjoys siding with you.
She clearly enjoys telling you this.
She did promise she'd be here at night if you don't arrest her... Good girl.
She doesn't even *understand* you asked her out. Perhaps you're too sober to pull it off right now? Try again later.
She enjoys talking about the company. It would not hurt to inquire more.
She genuinely wants you to, now. Don't make her ask.
She has fine-tuned her voice to find the most welcoming approach for attracting new customers. It doesn't work.
She is appraising you scientifically, the way she would an exceptional zoological specimen. It's not *like that*.
She is going to be over the moon. It will do you good to deliver some *good* news to someone for a change.
She is going to be over the moon.
She is resolute in this, yet you sense that she *wants* to tell you. You'll have to earn her trust first.
She likes you. The Crownhead is a boring condom. He's jealous. This is human nature.
She means your little talk before, after your breakdown. It's all right, she means to say. You returned the favour.
She means: you know her closer now, in some way you know her soul a bit.
She thinks she's a police officer... Try treating her like a police officer. A *lower-ranking* police officer.
She understands she has erred against the customer and immediately corrects course.
She wanted to talk to her, as they usually do. But she was brooding.
She won't judge you, no matter what you say.
She's a glib girl, but she liked the wording.
She's a negotiator. Just float a favour at her. Insinuate.
She's agreeing more and more. Partially because she *wants* to.
She's been holed up in here for a while with no one to talk to. Keep her talking, and you just might get an opportunity to break loose.
She's grown frustrated with her work and welcomes the opportunity to challenge authority in other ways.
She's locked up tight. What does she want? Maybe it's as simple as asking her. But whine about it so she feels superior.
She's making a joke. Her voice softens.
She's merely agreeing with you to reach the conclusion of this conversation faster.
She's not going to change her mind that easily. She still perceives you as a threat.
She's sentimental all right. You don't know enough about her to find out why exactly. She will keep her secrets for now.
Should you try *THE EXPRESSION* on her?
Simple. Just humiliate him in front of his entourage until he has to *beg you* in private to stop. Then you can let him know it was all but a clever ploy to separate him from his babes...
So much for subtlety. Better get straight to the point.
So that's how they met. This is *beyond* significant for them.
So that's what this is about.
So this is why Shanky doesn't like her. Useful for you -- it makes him advocate against Ruby.
Someone close to the victim might know.
Someone has to rush in to break the tension. The second in command.
Something changes between you two. She looks at you differently now -- as an equal. A fellow human being.
Something changes in his disposition. A merciful smile in the corner of his eye.
Sometimes, the most charming thing you can do is be *reasonable* in your requests.
Sounds like he cares about the place. He's not going to be overjoyed to hear that it's part of the Doomed Commercial Area.
Sounds like he wanted it to change her mind about the hanged man. This is definitely personal.
Sounds like she really didn't like those girls.
Sounds like she'd prefer her men to be less 'real'.
Sounds like she'll solicit your help with something -- when she gets the chance.
Sounds like the kid has a soft spot for Lilienne.
Start with when he first saw him? It will give him a chance to ramble.
Steal the Union boss's idea, add a twist, and present it as your own? Very capitalistic!
Still -- there is something there. She won't tell you now.
Still, after all this headache...
Stop making her angry! She won't start loving you again if you call her a war criminal. Seriously.
Stop making her angry. She won't start loving you again if you call her a *mass murderer*. Seriously.
Summon a smile on your face. A big shit eating grin like his.
Sure, she's all draped up in ironic detachment, but everyone's gotta eat.
Take it easy. You've broken her resistance. Pushing her further will gain nothing.
Tell her there's a *dead body* involved. That will make it easier.
Telling him is a REALLY bad idea.
Thank you for lightening his mood. It's hard down there.
That *is* true. People don't trust pretty people.
That I'm a queen. An extraordinary witness to grace.
That didn't go super well. You've gotta lay something better on her.
That didn't work? He must have sunk pretty low.
That is a way, *way* bigger compliment than it sounds like.
That is only a ludicrous daydream. Guess again. You're the...
That was a very smooth salesman's grin that almost comes off as earnest. You should learn from him.
That's a stone for leaders.
That's bad. You need to stop arguing, now. This won't be won by talking about the *case* -- they don't care.
That's probably for the best. Don't break your stride now.
The *boys* are too eager to please him to keep their mouths shut. You're gonna get a good head count here, just wait...
The beardless nature of your cheeks makes The Expression seem even more like a terrifying grimace.
The big one. Get this and they'll give her to you. Remember, don't piss him off. That never works.
The comfortably wealthy want their views challenged when making a purchase. Don't hold back.
The graciousness of her response makes you feel glad you didn't hassle her about her papers for no reason.
The ladies don't like that, it isn't *sexy*.
The lady is distressed. Perhaps something more up-beat might cheer her up?
The lieutenant clearly appreciates the chance to clear up the drummer issue himself.
The lieutenant doesn't appreciate your *sick charades* on his crime scene. His opinion of you has lessened.
The lieutenant doesn't really wanna go into this. Maybe let this one slide?
The lieutenant doesn't want to agitate the woman.
The lieutenant is undecided. On one hand he *wants* to shoot some gun -- on the other, it's an awfully stupid idea.
The lieutenant's face lights up. He doesn't usually notice these little things.
The lieutenant's opinion of you has *definitely* lowered.
The one with the bleached hair is not really pulling rank on his friend here. He's just trying to create the impression of himself as a reasonable, moderate leader figure.
The optimal way to go about this would be indifference. It begins by you telling him you don't care about any of this.
The segmented antennae move with apprehension, searching for something that's not there...
The segmented antennae move with apprehension, searching for something... you.
The smallest doubt takes root in him -- perhaps this cop is... no, it can't be. This has helped your standing in his eyes.
The tone somehow suggests to you that you're starting to become a little sponsored by him as well.
The voice coming through the whirlwind of pain is not malicious. She doesn't want to hurt you -- but she has to.
The words have already left your mouth...
There are crows feet in his eyes, he's laughing silently.
There has to be a way to resolve this situation peacefully.
There is a small measure of pride in her about this. That she could comfort and quell the rage in such a being.
There is a small measure of pride in her. That she could quell the rage in such a being.
There is curiosity there that goes unnoticed. The lieutenant takes a step back, absent-mindedly.
There is no way to sway this man in any direction. He is un-suggestable and un-swayable. Just tell the truth.
There is the tiniest bit of flattery in there -- the way her eyes move from your shoulder to shoulder.
There may be some way to get a drink out of him (even for you) but this is not it.
There was a speck of truth in there for him. You accidentally touched something.
There's laughter in the room. Try not to kill yourself this time.
There's no sweet-talking your way in there. Be official.
There's really no point in manipulating anyone. She'd be only too pleased to tell you about her work. Go on and ask!
There's really no point in manipulating anyone. You're chummy. Just go on and ask!
There's your in. Take his side in this particular fight.
These guys are so macho they're ready to confess to first-degree murder. Ask if it was them.
These ultraliberal types love losing huge sums of money on ludicrous proposals. Ergo, you should come up with a plan that's TOTALLY dead in the water.
They are filled with adoration and curiosity. The adoration of some wheel,  or dominion spinning around its parent deity. And the curiosity of a common wasp, tasting sugar in a fizzy drink.
They're leaving. They're all turning away from you.
They're not going to consider more than one piece of evidence -- choose wisely.
This Ruby is an old-lady whisperer. She knows how to work the village elder -- unlike you.
This definitely softened her up.
This guy knows Ruby, no doubt. Just play dumb for now. Get everything out of him you can.
This guy's your buddy-buddy. You feel it immediately: you belong to an organization. A fraternity. Of *drunks*.
This is as far as it goes with her. You'd need to put a year between you and your last drink for anything more.
This is going well for Measurehead.
This is the Horse-Faced Woman. I don't know *why* you named her that, but it was beyond idiotic. You should *never* address her using those words again.
This is the first time he openly acknowledges the kids' existence.
This is what is called a *conversation*. You don't have to be guarded right now.
This is where he tries to sow discord between you two -- because he knows the company is responsible for bringing the mercenaries into town.
This isn't going to change a lot of minds, Harry.
This isn't the place or time for questions: Who knows who might be watching from the distance, hidden behind the curtains.
This lady really should be a teacher. She's really good at the explaining things thing.
This man is not budging. Let's hope his superiors inside are more cooperative.
This man's got a hold over her. Even fifty years later, you can feel it...
This one knows how to keep things going. He's a hothead, but not one to be taken lightly. The last one is a bit of a black sheep...
This person Glen wants to hire -- he really respects her.
This person means you -- or no one else -- absolutely no harm. She will answer freely and honestly.
This pushed him, but not enough... just a little more...
This was a good move. Also, notice how Titus doesn't *like* her much. Especially when she's calling the shots.
This was not about failure or success. This was always going to be horror. I should not have suggested it, and you should not have listened to me.
This was your chance to make amends, but you chose not to. Instead you chose to be the bad-ass suicide boiadeiro and now he doesn't like you.
Time for a little truth. Tell the lieutenant you did some, clear the air.
Time to bring out your secret charm: tears and begging! Show him your *emotional* side, throw yourself before his very feet like a dog!
Time to get personal -- he's finally ready to open up to you.
Too late -- you already got a pretty good picture of these guys. You can use this.
Try as you might, you just can't fathom what a young rebel dressed like a granny would want from you. She certainly isn't a flowers and chocolate kinda girl.
Try it on Evrart!
Try not to sweat so profusely in her presence too. You'll blow your chances.
Try to force a tear out of your duct. Really *rip* into the whole emotional aggression thing.
Turn it around -- it's the seller who wields the power in this relationship.
Union boys are gonna help you *fix* it, he winks at you. Don't worry Harry.
Wait -- Let her come to it herself.
Wait! She doesn't want to share the space with them -- you can help her finish the research, then she won't have to! Offer to help.
Wait! she doesn't want to share the space with them -- help her finish the research, then she won't have to! Offer to help.
Wait, why don't you have a song?! Way to fuck it up! Better to try this line of questioning again after you've found the tape.
Was that a snarl or a suppressed groan? No love for you here.
Well buddy, you opened it. No need to go inside. It would be *rude*.
Well, now you've done it. Let's see if you can talk your way out of this one.
Well, well, bringing him that new bird sure made a difference in his attitude.
What *has* he done? Perhaps a confession will lighten the load...
What are you *doing?* Stop saying things like that.
What are you talking about? Don't say that, this is no time to nitpick.
What are you trying to be -- tactful? She wouldn't mind. Go on then...
What did I say? And what did you do? Damn it, man. You're buying it for twelve now, I guess.
What does she need? You don't have much to give her. Hmm, you could try offering her a job.
What he wants to know is how exclusive it is. The more untouched it is, the better.
What if I told you... it is actually possible to go on a date sober?
What is *wrong* with you? Why did you ask to be connected to her? Who *does* that?! Act professional now.
What is detective work if not an elaborate game? You need logical inference, attention to detail, the ability to analyse your opponents' motives...
What is it with these SKULLS and *cooperation*? They have to be the nicest violent street gang in Revachol West.
What is this, news of the world? Talk about *personal stuff*. Get to know him.
What just happened between these two?
What makes you think we *will*?
What was that about the Constabulatory before? You need to push some buttons if you want to recruit the lieutenant in a conspiracy.
What's more, all these girls seem to be Kojkos. What's up with that? You have to find out what's his deal with Graad...
What's with all the silences? It's like these guys don't know how to feel about this. You should keep picking at it...
What, does the lieutenant hate fun? Is he the *fun* police?
What? Jealous of the *Sorry Cop*? I think they'll be fine. Don't worry.
Whatever he may feel about you, he can't miss the opportunity to throw you a look of what he assumes is shared understanding. Go with it.
Whatever you do, do not to tell him you *know her*. That would sound *off*.
Whatever you do, don't make fun of the lieutenant for explaining this to you.
Whatever you're selling there -- he's buying it. In bulk.
Who cares what this Vicquemare said -- pull on their heart strings!
Who'd have thought you'd remember that nugget of wisdom after nearly frying your brain like you have?
Who, me?
Whoa, whoa. Did you see how easy that was? Ask her for more! *Toot-toot* Train to moneytown!
Whoa... that's so nice of him. Suspiciously nice.
With your feet trembling from the steps you took -- tepid and fearful -- you stand against her; her body close to you, radiating warmth. With your eyes closed you move your lips on her mouth.
With your feet trembling from the steps you took -- trepid and fearful -- you stand against her; her body close to you, radiating warmth. With your eyes closed you move your lips on her mouth.
With your help -- hopefully -- says her warm tone.
Wow, Kim's bad ass. *Or* he's had a druggie partner? Whoa. That's probably it! He's seen it before.
Wow. Okay. *Fuck off*? Maybe we were wrong about you then...
Yeah, maybe steer clear of the things reminding her of the 'floater' she used to be married to. Just saying.
Yeah... this just did nothing.
Yes! Somewhere hidden among the options popping up in your nervous system... is the *right* one. That will make her love you again, and not go to the aerodrome at all!
Yes, but even more importantly -- women. This specimen has surrounded himself not only with one or two, but *three* stellar babes.
Yes, but what was their relationship *outside* this unequal power dynamic?
Yes, falling out of line to *please* him! This is the best kind. And now this guy is going to...
Yes, it's *merciful* that way. It's your friend. Come back to it, we're all rooting for you to.
Yes-yes, impotent rage and lamentation. Let's wrap it up?
Yes. Back to cryptozoology. You don't want to sow disagreement between friends.
Yes. Guess what's yours?
Yes. Sorry Cop. The cop who's sorriest. Let's make it official, then, shall we?
Yes. This will be good. It will calm him down and won't cost any lives. Lives that could have been otherwise spared.
Yes. This will be good. It will calm him down and won't cost any more lives. Lives that could be spared.
You *feel* that she won't like the answer -- just as it's leaving your lips. Even more -- it will hurt her.
You can always talk your way out if things go sour.
You can coax it out of him, the lieutenant's preparing the ground.
You could get more out of him... He likes talking.
You could take another look at the tracks in the yard, see if they fit with the characters here -- it could be incriminating.
You didn't break this wagon fort, but you did manage to rattle the people inside a bit.
You don't know. Just be honest.
You got his attention. Now don't ruin it by making *weird* claims. Remember: you can still mess this up.
You have absolutely no idea. Familiar how? You must have forgotten something you heard again.
You have him off guard now... he's preoccupied with this situation with his woman.
You have no idea what the *usual* is. Just ask whatever comes to mind.
You know -- it might have been a *bad* idea to tell him that. Unless you want to be a drug baron and not a cop, of course...
You lost him. Quick!
You might be able to get on Garte's good side if you make up for the skua you broke?
You might be able to get on Garte's good side if you replace the broken skua you almost certainly broke?
You need to *contextualize* it for him first. Turn the key elsewhere -- the lock is here. It's here.
You need to ask him about specific cryptids -- cryptids you've heard about from Lena, or his friend Gary. He won't *just* talk.
You need to befriend him first.
You never had a chance. There was something off the moment you approached it. It was scared of you. Of humans.
You never had a chance. There was something off the moment you approached it... The creature didn't *like* you.
You ruined it. There was still a chance. You should have waited longer -- she would have called *you* instead!
You should ask her for a hint.
You should go with the flow. Join in on the experience.
You should thank him.
You should totally use emotional blackmail. Because that *always* works on women.
You stirred up some bad memories there.
You think she really needs more of that after her man died at sea?
You think you hear a sliver of accusation in her words.
You were good. Why did you have to get so creepy all of the sudden? Now it's just questions again.
You were warned not to bring it up. Your best shot here is to just back out of it.
You won't sweet-talk your way around this man. Good thing this psycho drug-boss is strictly optional.
You're a simple cop asking a simple question. He can't beat around the bush much longer.
You're an enthusiastic idiot. But you're still an idiot.
You're dealing with a subject near and dear to their hearts. It might behove you to tread *lightly*.
You're doing good here. Perhaps only for a moment, but still...
You're doing it! Despite your own best efforts you're still getting in, somehow...
You're in, but expect her to drive a hard bargain.
You're too likable to die. Don't worry.
You've got her. Now just reel 'er in.
You've got to work your way into this thing. The subtle way. Wind him up a little, don't immediately go for the kill.
You've hit a nerve. Titus is furious -- no, more than that -- the loyal Titus feels *betrayed*.
You've really worn down his patience. Even paying him didn't help!
Your deportment remains dignified -- as you shed a single, manly tear.
Your face, such as it is. A regular human face. Sans expression.

Your feet still tremble from the steps you took -- as if over an enormous distance -- standing against her; her body close to you, radiating warmth. With your eyes closed you move your lips on her mouth.
